shelfmark: Shelfmark string, e.g. Sinai Arabic 999 or Sinai Syriac NF X 1
summary: A summary of the manuscript. This is a prose description of the manuscript's contents, providing a brief overview suitable for display on the detail page. It can be a single sentence or a short paragraph.
extent: Extent of the manuscript, e.g. 210 ff.
weight: Weight of the manuscript, e.g. 1287.7 g
dim: Dimensions of the manuscript, e.g. 240 x 159 x 81.0 mm
coll: Collation formula string, e.g. Quire 1: 1x8 (8); Quire 2: 1x8+1 (17); Quire 3: 1x8 (25)

part.label: Part label, e.g. Part 1
part.summary: A summary of this part. Describes the support, date, script, and content of this physical production unit.
part.locus: Locus of this part, e.g. ff. 1-180
part.extent: Extent of this part, e.g. 180 ff.
part.dim: Dimensions of a typical folio in this part, e.g. 235 x 154 mm (average folio)

para.locus: Locus of the part-level paracontent, e.g. f. 1r
para.as_written: Part-level paracontent text as written in the original script
translation: English translation of the part-level paracontent
note: A note about this part-level paracontent item, e.g. introductory index added later.

note.value: This is a support note for the part. It describes the writing support material, e.g. ff. 1-10 are paper replacements; remaining folios are parchment.
This is a collation note for the part. It describes quire structure or signatures specific to this part.

related_mss.label: Label for part-level related manuscripts, e.g. related overtext layers
note: A note about this part-level related manuscript relationship.

mss.label: Label of a related manuscript at the part level
Label of a second related manuscript at the part level

ot_layer.label: Overtext layer label, e.g. Overtext layer (10th c., Naskh)

layer_record.label: Overtext layer label, e.g. manuscript name, part, and script description
layer_record.locus: Locus of the overtext layer, e.g. ff. 1r-85v
layer_record.summary: A summary of the overtext layer. Describes the script, date, language, and content in a brief prose statement.
layer_record.extent: Extent of the overtext layer, e.g. 85 ff.

writing.locus: Locus where this script is used, e.g. ff. 1r-85v
note: A note about this script, e.g. formal Naskh hand throughout.
A second note about the script to show the note field is repeatable.

writing.locus: Locus where a second script is used, e.g. ff. 1r-5v
note: A note about this second script entry, showing multiple writing instances.

ink.locus: Locus for this ink entry, e.g. ff. 1r-85v
note: A note about the ink, e.g. main text in brown iron gall ink.
A second note about the ink to show repeatability.

ink.locus: Locus for a second ink entry, e.g. ff. 1r-10v
note: A note about decorative ink colors used in headings and illuminations.

layout.locus: Locus for this layout entry, e.g. ff. 1r-70v
layout.dim: Writing area dimensions, e.g. 240 x 160 mm
note: A note about the layout, e.g. ruled in drypoint; text written below top line.
A second layout note to show the note field is repeatable.

layout.locus: Locus for a second layout entry, e.g. ff. 71r-85v
layout.dim: Writing area dimensions for the second layout section
note: A note about the second layout, e.g. layout shifts to two columns in final quires.

text_unit_record.label: Primary text unit label, e.g. name of the main text
text_unit_record.summary: A summary of the text unit content. This is a medium-length prose description that provides an overview of what this text unit contains, its language, and any notable features.
text_unit_record.locus: Locus of the text unit, e.g. ff. 1r-85v

work.orig_lang_title: Title of the work in the original language script
alt_title: First alternate title, e.g. title in original language
Second alternate title, e.g. title in another tradition
work.desc: A prose description of the work, providing context about its content, genre, transmission history, and significance. This field can accommodate a short phrase or a full paragraph.

alt_name: Second alternate name form, e.g. name in another language
agent_record.desc: A prose description of the agent, providing biographical context, dates of activity, and relevance to the manuscript tradition. This field can accommodate a short phrase or a full paragraph.

birth.value: Birth date as a human-readable string, e.g. ca. 850 CE

death.value: Death date as a human-readable string, e.g. 925 CE or 930 CE

floruit.value: Floruit date range as a human-readable string, e.g. late 9th–early 10th c. CE

note: This is a general note about the organization.

incipit.value: Incipit text of the work as a string value, e.g. the opening words in the original language
translation: English translation of the incipit
source: Source reference for this incipit, e.g. edition or manuscript

explicit.value: Explicit text of the work as a string value, e.g. the closing words in the original language
translation: English translation of the explicit

work_wit.locus: Locus of this work witness, e.g. ff. 1r-45v

excerpt.locus: Locus of the heading, e.g. f. 1r
excerpt.as_written: Heading text as written in the original script
translation: English translation of the heading text
note: A note about this heading, e.g. decorative heading in red ink

excerpt.locus: Locus of the incipit, e.g. f. 1v
excerpt.as_written: Incipit text as written in the original script
translation: English translation of the incipit text
note: A note about the incipit, e.g. standard opening formula

excerpt.locus: Locus of the first preserved line, e.g. f. 3r
excerpt.as_written: First preserved line as written in the original script
translation: English translation of the first preserved line
note: A note explaining why the text begins imperfectly, e.g. folio lost

excerpt.locus: Locus of the quoted passage, e.g. f. 20v
excerpt.as_written: Quoted passage as written in the original script
translation: English translation of the quoted passage
note: A note identifying the source of the quotation

excerpt.locus: Locus of the subscription, e.g. f. 85v
excerpt.as_written: Subscription text as written in the original script
translation: English translation of the subscription
note: A note about the subscription, e.g. colophon in red ink

excerpt.locus: Locus of the explicit, e.g. f. 85v
excerpt.as_written: Explicit text as written in the original script
translation: English translation of the explicit
note: A note about the explicit

excerpt.locus: Locus of the last preserved line, e.g. f. 85v
excerpt.as_written: Last preserved line as written in the original script
translation: English translation of the last preserved line
note: A note explaining why the text ends imperfectly, e.g. remainder lost

excerpt.locus: Locus of the prologue, e.g. f. 1r
excerpt.as_written: Prologue text as written in the original script
translation: English translation of the prologue
note: A note about the prologue

contents.label: Section label, e.g. Chapter 1 — short contents entry

note: A note about this section, e.g. marginalia or corrections present
contents.pref_title: Preferred title of a sub-work or section, e.g. used in contents work_id

note: A note about this work witness, e.g. translation tradition or textual affiliation.

translation: English translation of the supplication
note: A note about this supplication — this entry has two subtypes to demonstrate the multi-value subtype array.

location.repository: St. Catherine's Monastery of the Sinai

assoc_date.value: Origin date of the manuscript as a human-readable string, e.g. 10th c. CE
note: A note about this manuscript-level date, e.g. date of Part 1 main text block.

assoc_date.value: Origin date of a second part, e.g. 13th c. CE
note: A second assoc_date entry to show the field is repeatable, e.g. date of a later addition.

alt_name: Second alternate name form, e.g. name in another language
agent_record.desc: A prose description of the agent, providing biographical context, dates of activity, and relevance to the manuscript tradition. This field can accommodate a short phrase or a full paragraph.

birth.value: Birth date as a human-readable string, e.g. ca. 850 CE

death.value: Death date as a human-readable string, e.g. 925 CE or 930 CE

floruit.value: Floruit date range as a human-readable string, e.g. late 9th–early 10th c. CE

rel_con.label: Agent name as it appears in VIAF
Agent name as it appears in Library of Congress authority
Agent name as it appears in HAF
Agent name as it appears in Syriaca
Agent name as it appears in Pinakes

bib.citation: Full bibliographic citation string for a reference work about this agent.
Full bibliographic citation string. Second bib entry to show repeatability.

note: This is a first general note about the agent. Notes are plain strings and can be short or a full paragraph.
This is a second general note to show that the note field is repeatable.

cataloguer.message: Cataloguer message describing what was done in this edit.
Second cataloguer entry to show the field is repeatable.

assoc_name.as_written: Scribe name as written in the manuscript
note: A note about this scribe at the manuscript level, e.g. named in the colophon.

assoc_name.value: Free-text name value when not linked to an agent record, e.g. Unknown patron
note: A note about this patron, e.g. referenced in the colophon but name illegible.

alt_name: Second alternate organization name in another language
agent_record.desc: A prose description of the organization, providing context about its role in manuscript production or transmission.

rel_con.label: Organization name as it appears in VIAF
Organization name as it appears in Library of Congress authority

bib.citation: Full bibliographic citation string for a reference work about this organization.

note: This is a general note about the organization.

cataloguer.message: Cataloguer message describing what was done in this edit.

note: A third assoc_name entry to show the field is repeatable.

assoc_place.as_written: Place name as written in the manuscript
note: A note about this origin place, e.g. named in the colophon.

place_record.desc: A prose description of the place, providing context about its historical significance, geographic location, and relevance to manuscript production or transmission. This field can accommodate a short phrase or a full paragraph.

rel_con.label: Place name as it appears in an external authority file
Place name as it appears in Library of Congress authority

cataloguer.message: Cataloguer message describing what was done in this edit, e.g. initial description or update to existing record.
Second cataloguer entry to show the field is repeatable. Describes a subsequent edit or update.

note: A second assoc_place entry showing a repository event type.

place_record.desc: A prose description of the place, providing context about its historical significance, geographic location, and relevance to manuscript production or transmission. This field can accommodate a short phrase or a full paragraph.

rel_con.label: Place name as it appears in an external authority file
Place name as it appears in Library of Congress authority

cataloguer.message: Cataloguer message describing what was done in this edit, e.g. initial description or update to existing record.
Second cataloguer entry to show the field is repeatable. Describes a subsequent edit or update.

note.value: This is a foliation note. It describes the foliation system used in the manuscript, e.g. modern pencil foliation throughout; an older ink foliation partially visible in the outer margin.
This is a condition note. It describes the physical condition of the manuscript, e.g. binding intact but worn; front board detached; water staining on lower margins of ff. 100-120.
This is a binding note. It describes the binding structure, materials, and condition, e.g. leather over wood boards; remnants of fore-edge clasp.
This is a collation note at the manuscript level. It describes the overall quire structure or notes difficulties in establishing it.
This is a support note at the manuscript level. It describes the writing support across parts, e.g. ff. 124-129 are paper; the rest are parchment.

related_mss.label: Label for a disjecta membra relationship at the manuscript level
note: A note about this disjecta membra relationship.
A second note to show the note field is repeatable.

mss.label: Label of the first disjecta manuscript
Label of the second disjecta manuscript

related_mss.label: Label for a filiation relationship at the manuscript level
note: A note about this filiation relationship.

mss.label: Label of a second filiation manuscript
Label of a third filiation manuscript

viscodex.label: Label for the VisCodex visualization, e.g. Visualization of [Shelfmark]

bib.citation: Full bibliographic citation string for a reference work cited at the manuscript level.
bib.alt_shelf: [Alternate shelfmark from an older catalog, e.g. 652]
note: A note about this bibliographic entry, e.g. Atiya catalog reference.

bib.citation: Full bibliographic citation string. Second bib entry at manuscript level to show repeatability.
Full bibliographic citation string. Third bib entry to show multiple entries.

iiif.label: Alternate label for a second IIIF manifest, e.g. Reordered or MSI

cataloguer.message: Cataloguer message describing what was done in this edit, e.g. initial description or migration from legacy system.
Second cataloguer entry to show the field is repeatable. Describes a subsequent update.
Third cataloguer entry showing a minimal entry with no contributors.

program.label: Description program name
program.description: Full description of the program under which this record was created, including dates, funders, and project goals.

program.label: Second description program name, to show the field is repeatable
program.description: Full description of a second program that contributed to this record.

desc_provenance.rights: Rights statement for the descriptive metadata, e.g. CC BY 4.0.

program.label: Imaging program name, e.g. Sinai Library Digitization Project, Phase 1
program.description: Full description of the imaging program, including dates, funders, and project goals.
note: A note about the imaging process, e.g. automated processing techniques.
A second imaging note to show the note field is repeatable.
program.imaging_system: Imaging system description, e.g. BC100 - 2 cam., 960 ppi, 120mm lens

image_provenance.rights: Rights statement for the manuscript images, e.g. All images are property of St. Catherine's Monastery.

guest_layer.label: Top-level layer reference for guest content, e.g. Guest Content (Greek ownership note)

layer_record.label: Guest content layer label, e.g. manuscript name, part, and guest content description
layer_record.locus: Locus of the guest content layer, e.g. f. 1v or ff. 1v-4r
layer_record.summary: A summary of the guest content layer. Describes what has been identified, how it was added, and its relationship to the main manuscript.
layer_record.extent: Extent of the guest content, e.g. 4 ff. or f. 1v (partial)

writing.locus: Locus of the guest content script
note: A note about the guest content script, e.g. informal 13th-century minuscule hand.
A second note about the guest content script.

ink.locus: Locus of the guest content ink
note: A note about the guest content ink, e.g. main text in black; rubrics in red.

layout.locus: Locus of the guest content layout
layout.dim: Writing area dimensions of the guest content, e.g. 80 x 120 mm
note: A note about the guest content layout, e.g. written in available space on the flyleaf.

text_unit.label: Guest content text unit label
text_unit.locus: Locus of the guest content text unit

text_unit_record.label: Guest content text unit label, e.g. name of the guest text
text_unit_record.summary: A summary of the guest content. Describes what has been identified in the guest layer, which may be later additions, marginal notes, or content on reused material.
text_unit_record.locus: Locus of the guest content, e.g. f. 1v or f. 90r, margin

work.desc_title: Descriptive title for the guest content, e.g. Ownership inscription or Marginal note

work_wit.as_written: Guest content text as written in the original script
work_wit.locus: Locus of the guest work witness

excerpt.locus: Locus of the guest content incipit
excerpt.as_written: Incipit of the guest content as written
translation: English translation of the guest content incipit
note: A note about the guest content incipit, e.g. standard ownership formula
A note about this guest content work witness, e.g. added in a later hand.
A second note about the guest content to show the field is repeatable.

bib.citation: Full bibliographic citation for a reference work noting this guest content.

para.locus: Locus of the possession note within the guest content
para.as_written: Possession note text as written in the original script
translation: English translation of the possession note

assoc_name.value: Owner or institution name as a free-text value
note: Name of the owner as identified from the possession note
A note about this possession note, e.g. ink faded; partially illegible.
A second note — this para entry has two subtypes to show multi-value subtype array.

note.value: This is a note about the guest content text unit. Guest content records often describe the nature of the addition and its relationship to the main text.
This is a contents note for the guest content, describing what has been identified and what remains uncertain.

bib.citation: Full bibliographic citation for a reference work noting this guest content.

cataloguer.message: Cataloguer message describing what was done in this edit, e.g. initial description or migration from legacy system.
Second cataloguer entry to show the field is repeatable. Describes a subsequent update.

program.label: Description program name, e.g. Sinai Arabic Manuscripts Project
program.description: Full description of the program under which this record was created, including dates, funders, and project goals. This can be a long paragraph.

desc_provenance.rights: Rights statement for the descriptive metadata.

para.locus: Locus of the possession note in the guest layer
para.as_written: Possession note text as written in the original script
translation: English translation of the possession note

assoc_name.value: Owner or institution name as a free-text value

note: A note about this possession note in the guest layer.
A second note — this para entry demonstrates a multi-value subtype array.

para.locus: Locus of a reader note in the guest layer
para.as_written: Reader note text as written in the original script
translation: English translation of the reader note
note: A note about this reader note in the guest layer.

assoc_date.value: Origin date of the guest content as a human-readable string, e.g. 13th c. CE
note: A note about the guest content date, e.g. paleographic dating.

assoc_name.value: Annotator or owner name as a free-text value
note: A note about this annotator.
A note about the repository of the guest content.

place_record.desc: A prose description of the place, providing context about its historical significance, geographic location, and relevance to manuscript production or transmission. This field can accommodate a short phrase or a full paragraph.

rel_con.label: Place name as it appears in an external authority file
Place name as it appears in Library of Congress authority

cataloguer.message: Cataloguer message describing what was done in this edit, e.g. initial description or update to existing record.
Second cataloguer entry to show the field is repeatable. Describes a subsequent edit or update.

related_mss.label: Label for related guest content layers in other manuscripts
note: A note about the relationship between these guest content layers.

mss.label: Label of a related manuscript with similar guest content

note.value: This is a general note about the guest content layer. Guest content records describe additions, marginal notes, or content on reused material.
This is a condition note for the guest content layer, e.g. ink partially faded; text legible with difficulty.

bib.citation: Full bibliographic citation string for a reference work cited at the layer level.
bib.alt_shelf: [Alternate shelfmark from an older catalog, e.g. 652]
note: A note about this bibliographic entry

bib.citation: Full bibliographic citation string. Second bib entry at layer level to show repeatability.

cataloguer.message: Cataloguer message describing what was done in this edit.
Second cataloguer entry to show the field is repeatable.

program.label: Description program name
program.description: Full description of the program under which this layer record was created.

guest_layer.locus: Locus of the guest content layer

uto.label: Top-level layer reference for undertext, e.g. Undertext layer (5th c., Greek majuscule)

orig_date.value: Origin date of the undertext as a human-readable string, e.g. 5th c. CE
note: A note about the undertext date, e.g. paleographic dating.
